titleEn: What is Visha Chikitsa? Kerala's Ancient Toxicology
excerpt: Visha Chikitsa, or Agada Tantra, is the classical Ayurvedic science of toxicology. Learn how this rare hereditary specialty treats snakebite, scorpion sting and other envenomation.
contentEn:
Visha Chikitsa — known in the classical texts as Agada Tantra — is one of the eight branches of Ayurveda. It is the science of toxicology: the diagnosis and treatment of poisoning from animal, plant, mineral and artificial sources.

In Kerala, this knowledge has been preserved through hereditary lineages of physicians who treated snakebite, scorpion sting, spider and insect venom long before modern hospitals existed. At Sree Kayakalpam Vaidyashala, Vaidyar Shine Bhaskar carries this rare tradition forward, and has authored a Malayalam reference text on the subject.

Types of Visha

The classical texts distinguish between Sthavara Visha (toxins of plant and mineral origin), Jangama Visha (toxins of animal origin such as snakes and scorpions), and Gara/Dushi Visha (accumulated, slow-acting and artificial toxins). Each requires a different approach to neutralisation.

Treatment Principles

Agada Tantra combines emergency first-aid, herbal antidotes (agada formulations), purification procedures and dietary regulation. The goal is both to neutralise the toxin and to restore the body's balance afterward.

It is important to understand that for any venomous bite, modern emergency hospital care comes first. Ayurvedic Visha Chikitsa complements that care — guiding first response, supporting recovery and treating lingering effects of envenomation.

If you or a family member needs guidance, contact Sree Kayakalpam Vaidyashala for advice and follow-up care.
author: Vaidyar Shine Bhaskar

titleEn: Karkidaka Chikitsa — Why Monsoon is the Best Time for Ayurveda
excerpt: The Malayalam month of Karkidakam, during the monsoon, is considered the ideal season for Ayurvedic rejuvenation and detoxification. Here is why.
contentEn:
In Kerala, the monsoon month of Karkidakam (mid-July to mid-August) is traditionally devoted to health. Cool, humid weather opens the body's channels and makes it especially receptive to detoxification and rejuvenation therapies.

Why the Monsoon?

During the rains, the digestive fire (agni) fluctuates and the doshas — particularly Vata — become aggravated. The atmosphere is free of dust and heat, the pores are open, and the body responds well to medicated oils, herbal therapies and internal medicines. This is why Panchakarma and rejuvenation programmes are classically scheduled now.

What Karkidaka Chikitsa Includes

A typical regimen may involve Panchakarma purification, medicated kizhi (bolus) massages, Njavara rice treatments, special Karkidaka kanji (medicated rice gruel), dietary discipline and rest. The aim is to clear accumulated toxins, strengthen immunity and prepare the body for the year ahead.

Who Benefits

Those with joint pain, fatigue, recurring seasonal ailments or simply seeking preventive rejuvenation benefit most. A short consultation lets the Vaidyar tailor the programme to your prakriti and needs.

Book your Karkidaka programme early — slots during the season fill quickly at Sree Kayakalpam Vaidyashala.
author: Vaidyar Shine Bhaskar

titleEn: Kayakalpa Chikitsa: The Science of Rejuvenation
excerpt: Kayakalpa is Ayurveda's classical rejuvenation therapy, aimed at restoring vitality, slowing ageing and strengthening immunity. Discover what it involves.
contentEn:
Kayakalpa — literally the transformation (kalpa) of the body (kaya) — is Ayurveda's deep rejuvenation therapy, a branch of Rasayana (the science of longevity).

The Aim

Rather than treating a single disease, Kayakalpa works to renew the whole body: improving tissue quality, enhancing immunity, sharpening the mind and slowing the effects of ageing. It is rejuvenation in the truest sense.

How It Works

A Kayakalpa programme typically combines preparatory purification, carefully selected Rasayana medicines, a disciplined diet, adequate rest and lifestyle guidance. The regimen is always tailored to the individual's prakriti, age and strength.

Classical Rasayana herbs and formulations are chosen to nourish the dhatus (body tissues) and to support ojas — the subtle essence associated with immunity and vigour.

What to Expect

Programmes commonly run from three to six weeks. Patients often report better sleep, steadier energy, improved digestion and a greater sense of clarity and wellbeing. Because the medicines are prepared in-house using traditional methods, authenticity and potency are assured.

Speak with Vaidyar Shine Bhaskar to learn whether a Kayakalpa programme suits your needs.
author: Vaidyar Shine Bhaskar

titleEn: Understanding Your Prakriti: Vata, Pitta and Kapha
excerpt: Your prakriti is your unique mind-body constitution, set by the balance of three doshas. Understanding it is the first step to personalised Ayurvedic care.
contentEn:
Ayurveda teaches that everyone is born with a unique constitution — a prakriti — determined by the balance of three doshas, or bio-energies.

The Three Doshas

Vata (air and space) governs movement, breath, circulation and the nervous system. People with strong Vata tend to be quick, creative and energetic, but may experience dryness, anxiety or irregular digestion when imbalanced.

Pitta (fire and water) governs digestion, metabolism and transformation. Pitta types are often focused, sharp and warm, but may run toward heat, acidity or irritability when aggravated.

Kapha (earth and water) governs structure, stability and lubrication. Kapha types are typically calm, strong and steady, but may face heaviness, congestion or sluggishness when out of balance.

Why It Matters

Most people are a blend, with one or two doshas predominant. Knowing your prakriti helps the Vaidyar choose the right diet, lifestyle and medicines for you — because the same remedy can affect two people very differently.

A personalised consultation is the surest way to understand your constitution. Reach out to Sree Kayakalpam Vaidyashala to begin.
author: Vaidyar Shine Bhaskar

titleEn: Classical Ayurvedic Medicines Prepared at Sree Kayakalpam
excerpt: More than 100 classical formulations are prepared in-house at Sree Kayakalpam using the traditional wood-fire method. Here is what makes them authentic.
contentEn:
At Sree Kayakalpam Vaidyashala, medicine is not simply dispensed — it is made. More than one hundred classical formulations are prepared in-house, many over a traditional wood-fire cauldron, personally supervised by Vaidyar Shine Bhaskar.

Why In-House Preparation Matters

Classical Ayurvedic medicines depend on precise ingredients, timing and method. Commercial production often substitutes or shortcuts these steps. Preparing medicines in-house ensures the right herbs, the right proportions and the slow, careful processing the texts demand.

The Traditional Method

The wood-fire method allows gradual, even heating that classical recipes call for — drawing out the active principles of herbs into oils and decoctions without scorching. It is labour-intensive and slow, but it preserves potency and integrity.

The Range

The in-house pharmacy includes kashayams (decoctions), arishtams and asavams (fermented preparations), medicated oils, lehyams (herbal jams), gulikas (tablets) and specialised agada formulations for Visha Chikitsa.

This commitment to authentic preparation is at the heart of the Vaidyashala's parampara tradition — and a key reason patients trust its medicines.
author: Vaidyar Shine Bhaskar
